subject: [PATCH v3 2/4] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary:
Ritesh noted that the patch series can delay eviction of don't cache marked pages due to parallel buffered writers dirtying page cache pages, which may impact performance in certain workloads.

Reviewer noted that the performance benefits of IOCB_DONTCACHE are not evident in baseline cases, and suggested evaluating its impact on scenarios where buffered writers dirty page cache pages that can overflow RAM size, as this is where dontcache should shine due to reduced page cache pressure.

Reviewer Ritesh noted that the patch's change to kick off writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE may cause performance issues due to competition between parallel and buffered writes, particularly in nfsd's scattered I/O pattern.
reviews.raw_body:
The reason I am thinking of this is: dontcache marked pages, gets
evicted from page cache after they are written back. But this patch
series can now delay that from happening when there is a parallel
buffered writer dirtying page cache pages. Because of the reasons we
already discussed...

Note that, this may not be a workload which matters in the real world,
but I was thinking, it will be good to know the impact if any, of such
workload with this patch series (parallel buffered and dontcache
writers).

---

... If we see the performace of buffered and dontcache in baseline case,
then we don't see dontcache doing any good. Even the patched version is
just slightly better compared to buffered case.

But IIUC, dontcache should really shine in cases where we have buffered
writers dirtying the page cache pages which can overflow the RAM size
[1]. The reason why dontcache should show benefit there is, because we
don't see any page cache pressure, since after writeback the pages gets
evicted. Also earlier in the unpatched version, the I/O submission
happens immediately in the same context.

So, I guess, isn't it better to evaluate those scenarios as well with
the patched version - since this series affects those code paths now?

[1]: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20241110152906.1747545-11-axboe@kernel.dk/

---

Thanks!

-ritesh

2026-04-28.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE operations, which are used to prevent caching of writes. The change ensures that writeback is kicked off for these operations, similar to calling sync_file_range(2) with SYNC_FILE_RANGE_WRITE. This is a hint and does not provide hard guarantees on the state of the page cache. The patch addresses performance issues caused by competing I/O patterns, particularly when multiple threads write to the same file in parallel.

This is a hint and does not provide hard guarantees on the state of the page cache.
